title: Policy visions and lived realities diverge in pursuit of urban quality of life in Africa
data_sources: community quality-of-life interviews (seven SA low-income communities)

data_scope.study_design: constructivist grounded theory triangulating index, state and community perspectives
data_scope.policy_instruments: South African spatial-planning directives including SPLUMA
data_scope.data_availability: interview data restricted (confidential); not publicly shareable

core_conclusion: South African state planners and low-income communities broadly agree on what quality of life is, but frame and prioritize it differently across temporal, responsibility and spatial dimensions: communities need jobs, services and safety now, while the state plans for long-term development. These conflicting rationalities, shaped by interpretation, power and the uneven use of data, weaken policy and fuel protest.
why_it_matters: Reframes policy failure as a framing problem rather than a knowledge gap; grounds the abstract 'conflicting rationalities' debate in seven real communities; names the politics of uneven data use in spatial decision-making; highly relevant to the rapidly urbanizing Global South.
why_nature: Couples spatial planning to lived human well-being; centres equity in Global-South informal settlements; brings a rigorous qualitative grounded-theory method that complements the journal's quantitative urban science; directly policy-translatable.
added_value_to_my_research: Lived community priorities -> demand weights for 2SFCA accessibility/service-area delineation
Audit of 'uneven data use' -> explainable-ML feature attribution as a governance-transparency tool
Grounded-theory indicators fused with POIs/mobility/remote sensing -> spatially explicit QoL index (multi-source fusion, FAIR)
Actor-indicator conflict network -> community detection + agent-based modeling of state-community divergence
Cross-city transferability test -> agglomeration/regional-collaboration framing for transferable health-policy recommendations

related.relation: quantifies the infrastructure/QoL deficits the paper describes qualitatively

related.relation: geospatial deprivation mapping to scale the community-level divergence
